skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X with posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI. Apps: x/post-tweet, x/post-create (with media), x/post-like, x/post-retweet, x/dm-send, x/user-follow. Capabilities: post tweets, schedule content, like posts, retweet, send DMs, follow users, get profiles. Use for: social media automation, content scheduling, engagement bots, audience growth, X API. Triggers: twitter api, x api, tweet automation, post to twitter, twitter bot, social media automation, x automation, tweet scheduler, twitter integration, post tweet, twitter post, x post, send tweet
skill.summary: Automate Twitter/X posting, engagement, and user management via the inference.sh belt CLI.
skill.author: halt-catch-fire

security_audit.summary: The skill is a documentation file providing usage examples for the belt CLI to interact with Twitter/X via inference.sh. The 37 'external_commands' findings are false positives: they detect bash code blocks (```bash...```) containing belt CLI examples, not actual executable code. The 7 'network' findings are documentation URLs to inference.sh and X integration docs. The 2 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings at lines 3 and 29 are false positives caused by the scanner misidentifying markdown table syntax and description text as cryptographic code. The skill restricts execution to Bash(belt *) only, which is a security best practice.

low_findings.title: External CLI Execution (belt commands)
low_findings.description: The skill uses the belt CLI from inference.sh to interact with Twitter/X API. Execution is restricted via allowed-tools to Bash(belt *), which limits command injection surface. All inputs are JSON strings passed as arguments to belt subcommands.

low_findings.title: Network API Calls via belt CLI
low_findings.description: The belt CLI makes authenticated API calls to inference.sh endpoints which proxy to Twitter/X API. Authentication is handled by belt login, not by the skill itself. URLs in the documentation point to legitimate inference.sh domains.

content.user_title: Automate Twitter/X Posts and Engagement with Claude
content.value_statement: Manually posting, liking, and following on Twitter/X is repetitive and time-consuming. This skill lets Claude drive the belt CLI to post tweets, schedule content, send DMs, and manage engagement automatically.
actual_capabilities: Post text tweets and media tweets to Twitter/X via belt CLI
Like, retweet, and delete tweets by tweet ID
Send direct messages to users by recipient ID
Follow and unfollow users by username
Retrieve user profiles and tweet details by ID
Chain workflows like generating AI images or videos then posting them automatically
limitations: Requires the belt CLI to be installed and authenticated via belt login
Twitter/X API rate limits apply to all automated actions
Cannot interact with Twitter/X directly without the inference.sh proxy
DM functionality requires appropriate X API permissions and recipient IDs

use_cases.title: Content creators scheduling tweets
use_cases.description: A solo creator who wants to post promotional tweets, thread starters, and media posts on a schedule without manually logging in each time.

use_cases.title: Marketing teams running engagement campaigns
use_cases.description: A small marketing team that needs to like, retweet, and follow relevant accounts to grow audience reach and respond to community posts.

use_cases.title: Developers building AI-generated content pipelines
use_cases.description: A developer who generates AI images or videos and wants to auto-post them to Twitter/X as part of a content production pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Post a simple tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Post a tweet saying 'Excited to share my latest project update!'

prompt_templates.title: Post a tweet with media
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a futuristic city skyline, then post it to Twitter/X with the caption 'The future is now 🌆'

prompt_templates.title: Engage with a tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Like tweet ID 1234567890 and then retweet it to amplify the message

prompt_templates.title: DM a follower
prompt_templates.prompt: Send a DM to user_id 'abc123' saying 'Thanks for following! Here is the resource I promised: https://example.com'

output_examples.input: Post a tweet saying 'Hello world from Claude!'
output_examples.output: The skill invokes belt app run x/post-tweet with the JSON input. The CLI returns a success response with the new tweet ID and a confirmation that the post was published.

output_examples.input: Generate a sunset image and post it to Twitter
output_examples.output: The skill chains two belt commands: first generating an image via falai/flux-dev-lora, then posting it via x/post-create with the generated media_url. The result is a published tweet with the AI-generated sunset image attached.
The skill runs belt app run x/user-follow with the username parameter. The CLI returns a confirmation that the follow action was successful and shows the followed user's profile.

best_practices: Always run belt login first to authenticate before issuing any API commands
Use belt app sample to generate input.json templates for complex apps like x/post-create with media
Chain workflows by piping output from one belt command into the next for automated pipelines
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys or secrets in prompt inputs — belt handles authentication internally
Do not use this skill to spam or violate Twitter/X Terms of Service with mass follows or DMs
Do not bypass the belt CLI by calling the Twitter/X API directly — the skill is designed to work through inference.sh

faq.question: What is the belt CLI?
faq.answer: The belt CLI is a command-line tool from inference.sh that provides access to over 250 apps including Twitter/X integration. You install it via npx skills add belt-sh/cli and authenticate with belt login.

faq.question: Do I need a Twitter/X API key?
faq.answer: No. The belt CLI handles authentication through inference.sh, which proxies requests to the Twitter/X API. You only need an inference.sh account.

faq.question: Can I post images and videos?
faq.answer: Yes. Use x/post-create with a media_url parameter to post tweets with attached images or videos. You can chain this with AI image or video generation apps for fully automated content pipelines.

faq.question: What are the rate limits?
faq.answer: Rate limits are governed by Twitter/X API tier and the inference.sh plan. The belt CLI respects these limits automatically. For high-volume use, consider an X API Pro tier.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill restricts execution to Bash(belt *) commands only, meaning no arbitrary shell commands can be run. All API calls go through the authenticated belt CLI.

faq.question: Can I send DMs to any user?
faq.answer: DM functionality requires appropriate X API permissions. You need the recipient's user ID (not username) and your X API app must have DM access enabled.
